skill.name: sherpa-onnx-tts
skill.description: Local text-to-speech via sherpa-onnx (offline, no cloud)
skill.summary: Offline TTS wrapper using sherpa-onnx for local text-to-speech synthesis
skill.author: mylukin

security_audit.summary: All 65 static analysis findings are false positives from legitimate tooling patterns. The skill uses spawnSync to execute a controlled binary, reads environment variables for configuration, and performs standard filesystem operations. No evidence of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or command injection vectors found. This is a safe TTS wrapper tool.

content.value_statement: Convert text to speech files without internet access. Run local TTS synthesis using sherpa-onnx models with privacy and speed.
actual_capabilities: Convert text input to WAV audio files using local models
Support multiple voice models from sherpa-onnx TTS model collection
Run completely offline without cloud API calls or network dependencies
Handle model path resolution through environment variables or command flags
Create output directories recursively for audio file generation
Work across macOS, Linux, and Windows with platform-specific binary handling
limitations: Requires manual download and configuration of sherpa-onnx runtime binaries
Model files must be downloaded separately from GitHub releases
Output limited to WAV format only
Only processes text in languages supported by downloaded voice models
No streaming or real-time synthesis - generates complete audio files

use_cases.title: Accessibility Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate audio versions of documents for visually impaired users without sharing content with cloud services

use_cases.title: Offline Application Development
use_cases.description: Add text-to-speech features to desktop or embedded applications that must work without internet connectivity

use_cases.title: Privacy-Sensitive Audio Production
use_cases.description: Create voiceovers for confidential content where data cannot leave the local system

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text Conversion
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple text-to-speech conversion with default output file

prompt_templates.title: Custom Output Path
prompt_templates.scenario: Specify a custom output file location for the generated audio

prompt_templates.title: Model Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the {model_name} voice model to convert: {text}. Output to {output_path}
prompt_templates.scenario: Select a specific voice model for the synthesis task

prompt_templates.title: Batch Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these text segments to separate audio files: {segment_list}
prompt_templates.scenario: Process multiple text strings, creating individual audio files for each

output_examples.input: Convert this text to speech: Hello, world!
Generate speech audio at /tmp/greeting.wav from: Welcome to the local TTS system

best_practices: Set SHERPA_ONNX_RUNTIME_DIR and SHERPA_ONNX_MODEL_DIR environment variables before running TTS commands
Test with short text samples first to verify model installation and voice quality
Use absolute paths for model directories when running from different working directories
Download voice models that match your target language and desired voice characteristics
anti_patterns: Do not pass untrusted user input directly as text without sanitization
Avoid running with incorrect model directory paths - this will cause cryptic binary errors
Do not modify the runtime library paths unless installing sherpa-onnx to custom locations
Never attempt to use online TTS services when offline privacy is a requirement

faq.question: Why does the tool fail with binary not found?
faq.answer: The sherpa-onnx runtime must be downloaded and installed first. Run the installation steps in SKILL.md to download the runtime for your operating system.

faq.question: Can I use different voice models?
faq.answer: Yes. Download alternative models from the sherpa-onnx tts-models GitHub releases and set SHERPA_ONNX_MODEL_DIR to point to the new model directory.

faq.question: Does this require an internet connection?
faq.answer: No. After downloading the runtime and model files, all text-to-speech processing happens locally on your machine without network access.

faq.question: What audio formats are supported?
faq.answer: The tool outputs WAV files by default. The sherpa-onnx binary generates uncompressed WAV audio suitable for further processing or playback.

faq.question: How do I change the output file location?
faq.answer: Use the -o or --output flag followed by your desired path. Relative paths are resolved from your current working directory.

faq.question: Can I batch process multiple text strings?
faq.answer: Yes. Call the sherpa-onnx-tts command multiple times with different text inputs and output file paths to generate multiple audio files.
